{{Quote|The prisoners have captured the Fire Control Room. You must not allow them to damage the main cannon.|An Imperial officer during the [[Death Star Uprising]]|<ref name="BFII">''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]''</ref>}}
'''Superlaser Fire Control''', or the '''Fire Control Room''', was a chamber in the [[Theta Sector]] of both [[Death Star/Legends|Death Stars]] where the [[Superlaser firing station|superlaser controls]] were situated.<ref name="Episode IV" /><ref name="Episode VI" /> It was manned by [[Imperial gunner/Legends|gunners]] and [[Death Star Trooper/Legends|Death Star troopers]].<ref name="Episode IV" /> In the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]], the Fire Control Room was located in proximity to [[Detention Block 2180]] and [[Hangar 84G|Hangars 84G]] and [[Hangar 85G|85G]].<ref name="BFII" />

==Description==
[[File:Fire_Control_Room_BF2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Fire Control Room of the first Death Star.]]
Fire Control was present on both Death Stars.<ref name="Episode IV" /><ref name="Episode VI" /> It was responsible for coordinating the firing of all the tributary [[Superlaser/Legends|superlasers]] and controlling their collimation with a magnetic ring. The room contained eight [[Superlaser firing station|computers used to control the lasers]], along with four backup stations. In the first Death Star, the firing switch was located in Fire Control itself,<ref name="DSn" /> however, in the second battlestation, the firing switch was in the [[Death Star command center|main control room]].<ref name="Episode VI Novel">''[[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|Star Wars Episode VI: Return of the Jedi'' (novel)]]''</ref>

==History==
===The first Death Star===
Prior to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], a group of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] prisoners [[Death Star Uprising|escaped]] from Detention Block 2180 and took control of the Fire Control Room. The prison break was countered by the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] of the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]], who retook it.<ref name="BFII" />

[[File:Imperialgunner.jpg|thumb|left|200px|An [[Imperial gunner/Legends|Imperial gunner]] in the fire room]]
Later, [[Imperial gunner/Legends|Imperial gunner]] [[Tenn Graneet]] and his crew were placed in Superlaser Fire Control after they practiced for months in the [[Superlaser Simulator]]. When a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|Lucrehulk-class battleship]], the ''[[Fortressa]]'' appeared and launched fighters, Fire Control fired the superlaser at the Lucrehulk, destroying it. Eventually, they were given the orders to fire at the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Despayre]]. At only thirty-three percent power, the superlaser was required to fire three times to destroy the planet completely. Later, the crew were given orders to destroy [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]].<ref name="Episode IV" /> This act left Graneet, whose job it was to pull the firing switch, demoralized and full of resentment against the Empire.<ref name="DSn" />

Eventually, the Rebels [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|attacked the station]] after it found their [[Great Temple/Legends|base]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. Still reeling over Alderaan's destruction, Graneet did not pull the firing lever when the Death Star was in range, instead repeating to his crew, "stand by..." as he contemplated what to do. His hesitation gave [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], a Rebel pilot, enough time to fire [[proton torpedo]]s down a [[Thermal exhaust port/Legends|thermal exhaust port]], causing the Death Star to explode, destroying the Fire Control room and killing everyone in it, including Graneet.<ref name="Episode IV" /><ref name="DSn" />

===The second Death Star===
When the Death Star was rebuilt after the Battle of Yavin, the new station included a Superlaser Fire Control room as well. During the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], gunners in Fire Control targeted [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Rebel ships]], which were promptly destroyed by the superlaser. The gunners were forced to stop firing, though, when the Rebel fleet engaged the Imperial fleet at point blank range in order to not give the Death Star any ability to fire at the Rebel ships without hitting their own.<ref name="Episode VI" />

As the tide of the battle turned in favor of the Rebels, the crew was given orders to target the [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] itself if the Rebels managed to disable the shield generator.<ref name="Episode VI Novel" /> When Rebel [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] managed to blow up the [[Endor shield generator bunker|shield generator bunker]], the crew confirmed the deflector shield was down and began rotating the station towards Endor. When the station was only thirty seconds to firing, the Death Star exploded from damage done to its [[Reactor/Legends|reactor]] by the Rebels, destroying the Fire Control room and killing everyone onboard.<ref name="Episode VI" />
